title: Agent payments have a three-body problem
description: Who controls agent payments? A force simulation of the three-way gravitational fight between state regulators, card networks, and open protocols.
substackTitle: Agent payments have a three-body problem

title: U.S. GDP Status
description: What if LLM uptime was a macroeconomic indicator? A status page for the economy, where the components are AI providers and the metric is GDP.
favicon: data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 32 32'><circle cx='16' cy='16' r='14' fill='%23e5a00d'/></svg>

title: Agent-Era Infrastructure Map
description: Who's building the agent stack—and how open is it? Companies and protocols scored on openness and distribution across five infrastructure layers.

title: Payment Required
description: x402 requires Coinbase to verify and settle every payment. L402 requires no one. The protocol your agent uses decides who can shut it off.

title: The return of the computer
description: For three decades, infrastructure primitives got shorter—30-minute sessions to 10ms hops. Agents reversed the curve back to hour-long tasks.

title: Task complete. Safety violated.
description: BeSafe-Bench tested 13 production-grade agents. Not one cleared 40% safe task completion. The agents that performed best on tasks were the most dangerous in practice.

title: Payment rails for agents
description: Circle launched nanopayments claiming the financial layer of the agentic economy. On cost, every major payment rail is converging. On who can freeze a payment, they're not.

title: The AI market hyperscalers can't reach
description: Armada raised $230M to deploy modular AI data centers to military bases, oil platforms, and air-gapped facilities. The raise is a map of how far AI's addressable market extends beyond the cloud.

title: The forecast and the floor
description: Enterprise AI budgets grew nearly 6x. Gartner forecasts 40% of apps embedding agents by year-end. Actual production: 11%.

title: Where the 430× goes
description: AI token prices fell 430× in five years. But the same five years turned one-off chatbot queries into resident agents that burn 100× the tokens. For an always-on agent, most of that 430× never reaches the bill.

title: The corridor closes
description: Frontier API prices have fallen 430×. Open-weight alternatives fell faster. The margin corridor between what frontier providers charge and what the market will pay has collapsed from $29 to under $1—and frontier providers are now operating below their own cost floor.
